content: Hey, I'm trying to set up a new CI/CD pipeline for our microservices at work. The team is having trouble with build times. Any suggestions?
What cloud platform are you using? And what's your current setup like?
We're on AWS using CodePipeline and CodeBuild. The main issue is that our builds take about 15 minutes each, which is slowing down deployments.
Have you tried using build caching or parallelizing your builds? Also, what programming languages are you working with?
Mostly Python and Node.js. I've been meaning to set up caching but haven't gotten around to it. Between work and taking care of Milo, my new parrot, I barely have time for side projects.
Oh, a parrot! That's interesting. What made you decide to get one?
I adopted Milo from a shelter last month. He's a blue-and-gold macaw. I've always loved birds, and after my kids moved out, I thought it would be nice to have some company.
That's wonderful! Macaws are beautiful. Do you have any home automation set up for him?
Actually, yes! I've got automated feeders and a camera system so I can check on him when I'm at work. I'm pretty into home automation - it's one of my hobbies.
That's really cool! You could probably use some of those skills to improve your CI/CD setup too. Would you like some specific suggestions for reducing build times?
